etymology.description: 止(止) ('foot') + 一(一) ('beginning place') -> originally 㞢 ('to go') -> simplified to a point
etymology.interpretation: The original compound 㞢 depicted a foot 止(止) (zhǐ, 'foot') stepping on a line 一(一) (yī, 'one; the beginning place'), indicating the concept of 'to go'. The footprint evolving into a point symbolized direction or movement, ultimately abstracted into 之(之) (zhī, 'of; possessive particle'), representing directionality or connection, as in 'of'.

examples.chinese: 天空之蓝(天空之藍)让人心旷神怡(讓人心曠神怡)。
examples.pinyin: Tiānkōng zhī lán ràng rén xīnkuàngshényí.
examples.english: The blue of the sky makes people feel relaxed and happy.

examples.chinese: 树之影在湖面上摇曳(樹之影在湖面上搖曳)。
examples.pinyin: Shù zhī yǐng zài húmiàn shàng yáoyè.
examples.english: The shadow of the tree sways on the surface of the lake.
